blocks.body: Past Perfect vs Past Perfect Continuous — Past Perfect = had + V3 for a completed result or fact before another past moment, and Past Perfect Continuous = had been + V-ing for a process or duration leading up to another past moment.

{"level":"B1","intro":"Past Perfect usually answers what had happened or what was already true by then. Past Perfect Continuous usually answers what had been going on before then or how long it had continued up to that moment.","rules":[{"label":"PAST PERFECT","color":"blue","text":"had + V3 for a completed action, result, or fact that was already ready before another past moment.","example":"She had left before I arrived. / By the time the bus came, they had already found seats."},{"label":"PAST PERFECT CONTINUOUS","color":"emerald","text":"had been + V-ing for a process or duration that led up to another past event or moment.","example":"They had been waiting for an hour before the bus came. / He was tired because he had been working all day."},{"label":"KEY QUESTION","color":"violet","text":"Ask yourself: what had happened / what was already true by then? or what had been going on / how long before then?","example":"ready result before then / process or duration up to then"}]}

{"title":"1. Quick comparison","intro":"Both tenses look back from another point in the past, but the focus is different:","rows":[{"en":"Past Perfect","ua":"a completed result or fact before a past moment","note":"She had locked the door before she left."},{"en":"Past Perfect Continuous","ua":"a process or duration before a past moment","note":"She had been cleaning for an hour before she left."},{"en":"before / after","ua":"one action had already finished earlier than another past action","note":"After we had eaten, we went out."},{"en":"by the time","ua":"a ready result already existed before another past event","note":"By the time I arrived, they had finished."},{"en":"for / since / how long","ua":"duration or activity leading up to a past moment","note":"How long had you been waiting?"},{"en":"until then / before that","ua":"a process or period that continued up to that point","note":"Until then, he had been living with his aunt."},{"en":"already / just / never","ua":"often point to a completed result, experience, or fact before then","note":"She had already left. / He had never seen snow before that trip."},{"en":"result before then vs activity until then","ua":"had + V3 vs had been + V-ing","note":"I had repaired the bike. / I had been repairing the bike all morning."}],"warning":"If the result was already ready by that past moment, choose Past Perfect. If the activity or duration leading up to that moment matters, choose Past Perfect Continuous."}

{"title":"2. Forms grid","intro":"Compare the basic affirmative, negative, and question patterns.","items":[{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + had + V3: I had finished."},{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + hadn't + V3: She hadn't finished before lunch."},{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Question","subtitle":"Had + subject + V3? Had she already left before you called?"},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + had been + V-ing: I had been studying."},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + hadn't been + V-ing: They hadn't been sleeping well before the trip."},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","title":"Question","subtitle":"Had + subject + been + V-ing? How long had you been waiting?"}]}

{"title":"3. Where the focus changes the tense","sections":[{"label":"A ready result before that moment","color":"blue","description":"Choose Past Perfect when the action was already complete and the ready result, fact, or order of events matters. before, after, by the time, already, just, and never often point to this focus.","examples":[{"en":"By the time we reached the station, the train had already left.","ua":"The result was already true before that moment."},{"en":"After he had locked the shop, he went home.","ua":"First the completed action, then the other past action."}]},{"label":"Process or duration before that moment","color":"emerald","description":"Choose Past Perfect Continuous when the process or length of time matters. for, since, how long, until then, and before that often appear here.","examples":[{"en":"They had been waiting for forty minutes before the doctor called them in.","ua":"The duration before another past event is the main point."},{"en":"How long had she been studying before the exam started?","ua":"The question is directly about duration before that moment."}]},{"label":"Same situation, different focus","color":"amber","description":"The context can be similar, but the meaning changes: a ready result before that moment or a process continuing up to it.","examples":[{"en":"I had written the report before the meeting.","ua":"The report was already ready before the meeting."},{"en":"I had been writing the report until the meeting started.","ua":"The focus is on the process up to the meeting."}],"note":"Past Perfect often answers What had happened?, while Past Perfect Continuous more often answers What had been going on? or How long?"},{"label":"Cause of a visible past result","color":"violet","description":"Choose Past Perfect Continuous when an earlier process explains a state or result in the past.","examples":[{"en":"He was tired because he had been working since dawn.","ua":"The past tiredness is explained by the earlier process."},{"en":"The ground was wet because it had been raining.","ua":"The past result has its cause in the earlier activity."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Typical mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Using Past Perfect when the main idea is process or duration","wrong":"❌ She had cleaned the flat since 6 o'clock before the guests arrived.","right":"✅ She had been cleaning the flat since 6 o'clock before the guests arrived."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Mixing a continuous form with a finished result","wrong":"❌ I had been finished the report before lunch.","right":"✅ I had finished the report before lunch."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Using continuous with stative verbs","wrong":"❌ He had been knowing her for years before that.","right":"✅ He had known her for years before that."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"amber","title":"Using continuous for a ready number or total","wrong":"❌ By the time I checked, she had been writing five emails.","right":"✅ By the time I checked, she had written five emails."}]}

{"title":"5. Stative verbs and an important note","items":["know, like, believe, understand, want, and need usually stay in Past Perfect, not in Past Perfect Continuous.","Say I had known him for years before that, not I had been knowing him for years before that.","for and since do not automatically mean continuous: with states we often choose Past Perfect, for example They had known each other since school.","Past Perfect Continuous sounds most natural with actions and processes, not with every verb."]}

{"title":"6. A short decision guide","items":["Do you need a ready result, a completed action, or a fact that was already true by that past moment? → choose Past Perfect.","Do you have before, after, by the time, already, just, or never with a focus on what had already happened? → usually choose Past Perfect.","Do you have how long?, for / since, until then, or before that with a focus on duration? → choose Past Perfect Continuous.","Do you need to show that a past state or result came from an earlier process? → choose Past Perfect Continuous.","If the verb is stative, stay with Past Perfect even when the period continued up to that moment."]}

{"title":"7. Pages and related topics in this block","items":[{"label":"Tenses","url":"/en/theory/tenses","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect","url":"/en/theory/past-perfect","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/past-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect vs Past Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/tenses/past-perfect-vs-past-perfect-continuous","current":true},{"label":"Present Perfect","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect","current":false},{"label":"Present Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Present Perfect vs Present Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/tenses/present-perfect-vs-present-perfect-continuous","current":false}]}
